Antiquity Late Antiquity South Asian East Asian Islamic Jewish Scholasticism
Renaissance Early Modern
19th Century Phenomenology Analytic
Consciousness Science Machine Minds Moral Status
Glossary About
Empirical theories of experience · late 20th – 21st c.

Consciousness Science

Consciousness science gathers neuroscientific, affective, predictive, comparative, and psychoanalytic attempts to explain experience in empirical terms. The field ranges from global broadcasting and integrated information to emotion theory, animal consciousness, homeostasis, and the embodied predictive brain.

b. 1946

Bernard Baars

Global Workspace Theory: consciousness as what gets broadcast across the brain.

b. 1965

Stanislas Dehaene

The global neuronal workspace: a neuroscientific signature of conscious access.

b. 1960

Giulio Tononi

Integrated Information Theory: consciousness as intrinsic cause–effect structure.

b. 1959

Victor Lamme

Recurrent processing as the neural basis of phenomenal experience.

b. 1967

Michael Graziano

Attention Schema Theory: the brain’s model of its own attention.

b. 1972

Anil Seth

The predictive brain and consciousness as controlled hallucination of the body.

b. 1961

Mark Solms

Affect-first consciousness: the brainstem, feeling, and neuropsychoanalysis.

contemporary

Friston, Hohwy & Clark

The predictive-processing constellation: free energy, active inference, and the embodied mind.

late 20th c.

Varela, Thompson & Rosch

The Embodied Mind: enactivism, autopoiesis, and neurophenomenology.

b. 1957

Andy Clark

The extended mind, the predictive brain, and an embodied account of cognition.

b. 1963

Lisa Feldman Barrett

Constructed emotion, allostasis, and the predictive social brain.

1897 – 1979

Wilfred Bion

Thinking, containment, and the emotional metabolism of experience.

1871 – 1945

Walter B. Cannon

Homeostasis, emotion, and the body's nonconscious wisdom.

b. 1944

Antonio Damasio

Self, feeling, and consciousness as organismic regulation made explicit.

1915 – 2003

Donald Griffin

Animal consciousness and the scientific rehabilitation of minded behavior.

1882 – 1960

Melanie Klein

Internal objects, phantasy, and the earliest structure of mental life.

b. 1949

Joseph LeDoux

Survival circuits, conscious fear, and the layered architecture of emotion.

1903 – 1989

Konrad Lorenz

Ethology, instinct, and the biological shaping of minded action.

1943 – 2017

Jaak Panksepp

Affective neuroscience and the primal emotional systems of the brain.

1907 – 1988

Nikolaas Tinbergen

Ethology and the biological analysis of purposive behavior.

1859 – 1947

Pierre Janet

Dissociation, conduct, and the layered organization of conscious life.

1856 – 1939

Sigmund Freud

Conflict, repression, and the unconscious depth structure of psychic life.

1948 – 2024

Frans de Waal

Primate cognition, emotion, and the continuity between human and animal minds.

1896 – 1971

D. W. Winnicott

Psyche-soma development, transitional space, and the relational origins of selfhood.

b. 1950

Michael Tomasello

Shared intentionality and the social-developmental emergence of the human mind.

contemporary

Giovanna Colombetti

Enactive affectivity, living bodies, and emotionally structured sense-making.

Digital Minds · A history of thinking about the mind, from antiquity to the present day.
A personal research project by Fabián. · About · Glossary
